questions.question: For 1 cm gap in air at 760 mm pressure and 20°C temperature the Break Down Voltage is -

explanations.a: Incorrect. That is too low for 1 cm gap.
explanations.b: Correct. Paschen's curve gives approximately 30.3 kV for 1 cm gap at standard conditions.
explanations.c: Incorrect. 22.92 kV is for a different gap distance.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Too high for 1 cm gap.

questions.question: Sphere gaps are used to measure -

answers.c: DC, AC peak and impulse voltages
answers.d: Only DC and AC peak voltage

explanations.a: Partial answer. Sphere gaps measure more than just DC.
explanations.b: Partial answer. They also measure DC and impulse.
explanations.c: Correct. Sphere gaps are standard for measuring DC, AC peak, and impulse voltages.
explanations.d: Incorrect. They also measure impulse voltages.

questions.question: Sphere gap BDV measurement is linear and valid for gap spacing less than -

answers.d: Two times the diameter of sphere

explanations.a: Correct. For accurate measurements, gap spacing should be less than the sphere radius to maintain uniform field.
explanations.b: Incorrect. That would cause field distortion.

questions.question: In volt-time characteristics, the breakdown voltage for a negative impulse is _______ the positive impulse.

explanations.a: Correct. For non-uniform fields, negative impulse breakdown voltage is generally less than positive impulse due to space charge effects.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Negative impulse typically has lower breakdown voltage.

questions.question: The main factors that affect the spark over voltage of sphere gap are -

answers.b: Nearby earthed objects and atmospheric conditions

explanations.b: Correct. Nearby earthed objects distort the field, and atmospheric conditions (pressure, temp, humidity) affect breakdown.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Waveform is not a primary factor for sphere gap calibration.
